issue: Numeric boundary leak: 70–99% (c1) and 50–69% (c2) are adjacent ranges; hiding c1 leaves c2 visible, trivially deriving the boundary at 70%.
Paired single-fact split: c1 (0.25 mg/kg) and c2 (25 mg max) are two inseparable components of one dosing rule; together they define the complete dose.
Paired single-fact split: HIT duration rule splits into two inseparable halves (3 months vs 3–6 months)
Paired single-fact split: Rivaroxaban HITT dosing rule splits into two inseparable halves (15 mg BID × 3 weeks then 20 mg daily)
Mutually-exclusive list elimination: ITP classification spans 3 mutually exclusive duration categories; revealing two leaves third trivially
Numeric boundary leak: c1 specifies SBP <90 mm Hg while c2 specifies ≥15 minutes; these define a single hemodynamic criterion together
Paired single-fact split: Apixaban cancer-VTE dosing rule is one inseparable regimen (10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID)
Paired single-fact split: Rivaroxaban cancer-VTE dosing rule is one inseparable regimen (15 mg BID × 21 days then 20 mg daily)
Numeric boundary leak: c1 specifies '2.0–2.9×' which directly constrains the range, and hiding c1 leaves the multiplier visible in context
Numeric boundary leak: c2 threshold of <45 is mentioned alongside c1's <60; hiding c1 leaves a related threshold visible
Numeric boundary leak: c1 threshold (≥2.5 years) and c2 threshold (<1 year) are mutually related decision boundaries for the same clinical parameter (life expectancy)
Numeric boundary leak: c1 contains two related thresholds (≥100 AU and ≥75th percentile) and c2 (≥1000 AU) builds on c1's scale, making the relationship transparent
List elimination: c1 and c2 are intervals from a 3-item screening strategy list where naming the intervals trivially identifies which strategy is being asked about on cards 2 and 3
Numeric boundary leak: c1 answer (100) appears as threshold in c2 context (≤50 in CKD stage ≥3); hiding c1 leaves the 50 mg dose visible, allowing user to infer the normal value difference.
Paired single-fact split: the Euro-Lupus regimen is one inseparable protocol (500 mg IV every 2 weeks × 6 = 3 g total); splitting dose interval from total dose violates the single-fact principle.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 (≥30 mg/day) and c2 (≥5 gm in 1 year) are interdependent thresholds defining very high-dose GC use; hiding one leaves the other visible to infer the missing bound.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 has both 500 and 250 mg; c2 has 10 and 5 mg/kg. The 2:1 and 1:1 ratios between weight categories (>45 kg vs <45 kg) and dosing make the dosing scheme interdependent.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 defines <200 copies/mL; c2 specifies 20–200 copies/mL. Hiding c1 leaves the upper bound of c2 visible (200), directly revealing the c1 threshold.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is 50 mg twice daily; c2 is 50 mg once daily. Hiding c1 leaves the c2 dosing visible, which directly reveals that c1 must be double the daily amount.

Numeric boundary leak: c1 is <100,000/µL and c2 is ≥10 mmHg. On the card hiding c1, the visible text shows 'HVPG ≥10 mmHg' and other surrogates including 'varices, splenomegaly,' making it clear that c1 refers to a platelet count threshold. The ≥10 mmHg visible on a card hiding c2 doesn't directly leak c2's value, but the structure is independent thresholds for different measures.
